title: papaver radicatum var. pygmaeum
intro: Papaver radicatum var. pygmaeum, also known as the dwarf poppy, is a small, perennial plant species that belongs to the poppy family. This plant is rich in medicinal properties, particularly containing alkaloids such as meconic acid, which has been traditionally used to treat various health issues. The dwarf poppy has been shown to possess anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, providing potential health benefits including reducing stress, improving sleep, and alleviating pain. Traditionally, the plant's rhizomes and roots are used in herbal preparations, often in the form of tinctures or infusions, to take advantage of its medicinal properties.
